Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Show HN: Claude skill that evaluates B2B vendors by talking to their AI agents 24 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Personal Encyclopedias 656 upvotes, 131 comments
  3. Optimization lessons from a Minecraft structure locator 47 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. Obsolete Sounds 155 upvotes, 32 comments
  5. More precise elevation data for GraphHopper routing engine 76 upvotes, 13 comments
  6. OpenTelemetry profiles enters public alpha 37 upvotes, 5 comments
  7. Light on Glass: Why do you start making a game engine? 17 upvotes, 1 comments
  8. Interoperability Can Save the Open Web 135 upvotes, 34 comments
  9. My DIY FPGA board can run Quake II 209 upvotes, 62 comments
  10. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 783 upvotes, 269 comments
  11. Government agencies buy commercial data about Americans in bulk 220 upvotes, 71 comments
  12. From zero to a RAG system: successes and failures 207 upvotes, 67 comments
  13. My home network observes bedtime with OpenBSD and pf 55 upvotes, 13 comments
  14. Intel Announces Arc Pro B70 and Arc Pro B65 GPUs 78 upvotes, 28 comments
  15. SpaceStarCarz KoolWheelz Paper Models 20 upvotes, 4 comments
  16. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 380 upvotes, 178 comments
  17. Moving from GitHub to Codeberg, for lazy people 323 upvotes, 156 comments
  18. Stripe Projects: Provision and manage services from the CLI 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  19. Earthquake scientists reveal how overplowing weakens soil at experimental farm 203 upvotes, 108 comments
  20. End of "Chat Control": EU parliament stops mass surveillance 320 upvotes, 188 comments
  21. Niche Museums 81 upvotes, 39 comments
  22. Olympic Committee bars transgender athletes from women’s events 30 upvotes, 11 comments
  23. My minute-by-minute response to the LiteLLM malware attack 74 upvotes, 36 comments
  24. Swift 6.3 257 upvotes, 156 comments
  25. ARC-AGI-3 472 upvotes, 304 comments
  26. Building a Blog with Elixir and Phoenix 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. LibreOffice and the Art of Overreacting 173 upvotes, 109 comments
  28. What came after the 486? 126 upvotes, 108 comments
  29. French e, è, é, ê, ë – what's the difference? 73 upvotes, 64 comments
  30. Ashby (YC W19) Is Hiring Engineers Who Make Product Decisions 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 1:30am, 27 March 2026. Source here.
